page: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Esv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place the E64-16s_v6 in the average to strong performance tiers. Single-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2115.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.31 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is solid, yielding a Geekbench multi-core score of 12457.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 15978.86. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only cached small-block bandwidth of 1241.41 GB/sec and an uncached large-block write bandwidth of 90.18 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves 22.37 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and demonstrates capable small-model LLM text generation at 288.15 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the E64-16s_v6 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its massive 512.0 GB memory allocation relative to its 16 vCPUs. However, the lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external Premium Storage volumes for persistent data. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive database operations, large-scale caching layers like Redis, enterprise resource planning systems, and in-memory data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 4.2 GHz with a 260 MB L3 cache, this x86_64 instance supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. It features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2115.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.31 GB/sec, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. Qualitatively, the instance provides excellent resource density for memory-heavy applications, offsetting the lack of local storage. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 4.2 GHz speed, built for in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a massive RAM-to-vCPU ratio and Intel Xeon performance for enterprise database workloads.

Massive 512.0 GB RAM configuration providing 32.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions
